learning_objective: Compare and contrast the three cerebral white matter tracts (i.e., association, commissural, projection)
question: Compare and contrast the three cerebral white matter tracts (i.e., association, commissural, projection)
answer: The organising idea is that the three white matter tracts are defined by what they connect, so association tracts link regions within a hemisphere, commissural tracts link the two hemispheres, and projection tracts link the cortex with lower structures. Connection target names the tract. This idea of naming by connection frames the comparison.
explanation:
- The cerebral white matter has three kinds of tract.
- Association tracts connect regions within the same hemisphere.
- Commissural tracts connect the two hemispheres.
- Projection tracts connect the cortex with lower structures such as the brainstem and cord.

learning_objective: Compare and contrast the three cerebral white matter tracts (i.e., association, commissural, projection)
question: Explain: Association Tracts
answer: Begin with the tracts within a hemisphere, linking its own regions. These are the association tracts.
explanation:
- Association tracts connect different regions within the same cerebral hemisphere.
- They link cortical areas so that they can work together.
- They allow, for example, sensory and motor areas on one side to coordinate.
- They keep communication within a single hemisphere.
